name: Manufacturing Africa
description: Manufacturing Africa supports green manufacturing start-ups in Africa with strategy, investor access, and fully funded transaction advisory services to accelerate sustainable manufacturing across the continent.

## Accelerating sustainable
## manufacturing across Africa
[](https://manufacturingafrica.org/accelerator/)
![](https://manufacturingafrica.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/startup-1.svg)
#### Start Ups
We support green manufacturing start-ups in Africa with strategy and investor access to scale impact to grow the green manufacturing ecosystem.
[](https://manufacturingafrica.org/for-manufacturers/)
![](https://manufacturingafrica.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/manufacture-1.svg)
#### Manufacturers
We provide fully funded transaction advisory services to fast-track investment and help secure the capital you need for expansion.
[](https://manufacturingafrica.org/for-investors/)
![](https://manufacturingafrica.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/investor-1.svg)
#### Investors
Discover high-potential, de-risked manufacturing ventures ready for investment and get first access to growth engines across the continent.

#### IMPACT STORIES
### A vibrant manufacturing environment, unlocking extensive opportunities
Africa’s population is projected to double by 2050, and the working population is expected to increase by 800 million. Africa will need to create approximately 18 million jobs annually to provide sufficient employment opportunities for this population. The manufacturing sector has the potential to provide millions of better and more inclusive jobs across the continent, while building resilient economies. Over the past five years, Manufacturing Africa has been dedicated to unlocking this potential. Supported by UKaid from the UK government, we provide expert assistance that turns ambition into tangible, sustainable growth.
# 5
# Years of
Manufacturing
in Africa
#### DEALS CLOSED SPOTLIGHT
Supporting Ethiopia’s
Food Security
Ethiopia's largest consumer goods platform secured a £17.25 million investment to build a new edible oil plant. This major expansion will create over 200 new jobs and support the livelihoods of up to 7,000 smallholder farmers through local sourcing of raw materials.
_Read More_
[](https://manufacturingafrica.org/documents/supporting-ethiopias-food-security/)
Growing a Kenyan Household Name
Glacier Products, home to the popular Dairyland brand, secured **£4.93 million** to expand its facilities and diversify its product range. This investment will create **50 direct and 500 indirect jobs** while strengthening the local dairy farming community._Read More_
[](https://manufacturingafrica.org/documents/growing-a-kenyan-household-name/)
Boosting Pharmaceutical Production in Kenya
Leading regional manufacturer Regal Pharmaceuticals raised **£6.57 million** to develop a new facility dedicated to penicillin-based products. Our support in creating a solid financial model was crucial in securing the deal, which will greatly decrease the region's dependence on imports._Read More_
[](https://manufacturingafrica.org/documents/boosting-pharma-production-in-kenya/)
Powering Nigeria’s
Energy Independence
Arnergy, a provider of technology-enabled solar solutions, secured **£14.79 million** in its Series B funding round. Our work validating their growth strategy was crucial in helping them expand their clean energy services for commercial and industrial clients._Read More_
[](https://manufacturingafrica.org/documents/powering-nigerias-energy-independence/)
Pioneering E-Waste Solutions in Nigeria
Hinckley Associates raised**£3.70 million** to expand its e-waste management facilities, including Africa's first lithium-ion battery recycling plant. Our investor outreach was vital for securing investment in this landmark facility, which will generate **100 green jobs**._Read More_
[](https://manufacturingafrica.org/documents/pioneering-e-waste-solutions-in-nigeria/)
Enhancing Rwanda’s Agricultural Productivity
A European fertiliser manufacturer raised**£8.22 million** to expand its production of an environmentally friendly biostimulant in Rwanda. Our support in developing a comprehensive business plan helped secure the investment, which will increase crop yields for local farmers._Read More_
[](https://manufacturingafrica.org/documents/enhancing-rwandas-agricultural-productivity/)
Improving Food Security
in Rwanda and DRC
A leading Rwandan animal feed producer secured**£2.46 million** to double its production capacity and expand into aquafeed. Our commercial diligence was crucial in preparing the company to raise this capital, which will create 60 new jobs and support **26,000 smallholder farmers**._Read More_
[](https://manufacturingafrica.org/documents/improving-food-security-in-rwanda-and-drc/)
Powering Kenya’s
E-Mobility Transition
Roam, a leader in electric mobility, secured a**£19.72 million** Series A funding round to expand the production of electric motorcycles and buses in Kenya. Our market analysis was essential in refining their expansion strategy and strengthening their compelling case for investors._Read More_
[](https://manufacturingafrica.org/documents/powering-kenyas-emobility-transition/)
Building Ethiopia’s
Green Future
The innovative startup Kubik secured **£2.80 million** in seed funding to convert hard-to-recycle plastic into affordable, low-carbon building materials. Its new factory in Ethiopia will generate **170 direct jobs** and empower thousands of local waste collectors, turning an environmental challenge into an economic opportunity._Read More_
[](https://manufacturingafrica.org/documents/building-ethiopias-green-future/)
Innovative Cooling
for Off-Grid Nigeria
Koolboks, a renewable energy refrigeration company, raised **£2.63 million** to expand its range of pay-as-you-go solar-powered freezers. Our support in developing their market assessment was crucial in making these essential cooling solutions more accessible to businesses and communities across Africa._Read More_
[](https://manufacturingafrica.org/documents/innovative-cooling-for-off-grid-nigeria/)
